clues.clue: This envious lion brother murders Mufasa to seize Pride Rock.
This sea witch tricks Ariel out of her voice in exchange for legs.
This fashion-obsessed fiend wanted to skin Dalmatian puppies for a coat.
This royal vizier in Agrabah carried a snake-headed staff and a parrot named Iago.
This self-styled 'Mistress of All Evil' cursed Princess Aurora to prick her finger.
This yellow electric mouse is the franchise mascot of Pokémon.
A 2003 Weebl flash cartoon endlessly chants this woodland animal's name (with "mushroom mushroom").
Released in North America in 2002, this GameCube life-sim is full of talking animal villagers.
This 2007 site captioned cat photos with misspelled gems like "I Can Has Cheezburger?"
clues.response: What is I Can Has Cheezburger? (lolcats)

clues.clue: This 2007 viral video paired a piano-"playing" feline with the phrase "play him off."
The zany Warner siblings were Yakko, Wakko and this little sister.
These two lab mice, one a genius and one insane, schemed nightly to take over the world.
clues.response: Who are Pinky and the Brain?

clues.clue: This 1992 animated series starred the Dark Knight of Gotham and his butler Alfred.
This clawed, gruff X-Men hero has a healing factor and an adamantium skeleton.
Tiny Toon Adventures and Animaniacs were both produced by this blockbuster filmmaker, Steven.
Three sugar-and-spice sisters battled the simian villain Mojo Jojo.
This lab-coated boy genius hid a secret laboratory from his sister Dee Dee.
This buff, pompadour-sporting ladies' man in shades constantly struck out with women.
Ed, Edd n Eddy ran scams to buy these spherical, jaw-straining candies.
This cowardly pink dog protected elderly owners Muriel and Eustace from the paranormal.
The duo who recorded the 'Friends' theme were this two-piece pop band.
The actor who sings the 'Fresh Prince' rap theme also stars in the show.
The Jeffersons and All in the Family were both created by this producer, first name Norman.
Modern streaming shows added a 'skip' button mainly to bypass these recurring opening songs.
The brief, wordless 'Office' (U.S.) theme is played mainly on this keyboard instrument.

final.clue: For decades, cereal boxes hid these small plastic surprises that kids would dig through the flakes to find.
